_readme: Contract/payroll terms — the ONE place these numbers live. generate_report.py
refuses to compute a salary figure for anyone whose config has a null value it
needs, rather than silently defaulting to 0 or guessing.
Every non-null number below was copied verbatim from
(the June 2026 audit you shared) — re-confirm these are still correct before
relying on this for a new pay period, especially after probation ends or any
valid_call_definition is deliberately null: the June audit itself flagged this
as UNRESOLVED ('please confirm before payout') — it changes part-time pay by
roughly 6x depending on the answer. Set it to 'connected_45s' or

period._note: start_date/end_date in YYYY-MM-DD. excluded_weekdays are dropped from every working-day count, matching the June audit's 'Sundays excluded' rule.

order_incentive_tiers._note: Rupees, per the customer's order sequence with THIS company (not per rep). Source: Clause 10 / 6.2.

order_incentive_qualification._note: An order incentive is only 'earned' (vs. pending) once a customer reaches their 3rd order AND that order is >= this value. Source: Clause 10.

target_bonus._note: A rep needs more than this many qualified customers (3rd order + min_order_value) in the period to earn any target bonus. Amount is per-rep, see employees below.

employees._note: Key = canonical_name exactly as it appears in the reps table (run rebuild_rep_directory.py first if unsure). rep_sim_number is filled in automatically by generate_report.py from the reps table — leave it null here.

discipline_rules._note: Only applied to employees with discipline_rules_applicable=true (full-time, per the June audit).

gold_lead._note: First outgoing call to a Gold Lead must land within this many minutes of lead assignment (callyzer_leads.created_date) to 'pass'. June audit marks this PROVISIONAL — the Gold Lead tag carries a date but no time, so the check is indicative only until Callyzer exports assignment time precisely.
